data.title: Stocks Sell Off as Economic Risks of Iran War Build — WSJ
data.description:
By Hannah Erin Lang

The economic risks of the war in Iran are getting real on Wall Street.

U.S. stock indexes tumbled on Thursday, after it became clear to investors that Iran was willing to inflict — and suffer — economic pain as the conflict in the Middle East drags on. The Dow dropped 1.6%, or 739 points, while global oil prices logged their largest one-day percentage gain since the Covid pandemic.

"Reality is finally setting in," said Chris Grisanti, chief market strategist at MAI Capital Management. Ramping up production of oil and gas "is going to take weeks — even in the best scenario, even if the war ended tomorrow."

"I've been surprised by the complacency of the equity market," he added. "I think that's finally started to go away."

In his first official message as Iran's new supreme leader, Mojtaba Khamenei vowed to continue fighting and said his country will keep the Strait of Hormuz closed — blocking a crucial route for oil and gas shipments. A number of ships have been attacked in and near the strait.

And President Trump said Thursday that stopping the Iranian regime from getting nuclear weapons was a higher priority than oil prices.

Brent crude futures, the global benchmark for oil prices, jumped 9.2% to $100.46 a barrel, their largest one-day gain since May 2020. Stocks fell in tandem: The S&P 500 retreated 1.5%. The Nasdaq dropped 1.8% and is now down roughly 7% from its most recent high.

Shares of energy companies like Occidental Petroleum and Phillips 66 climbed, as did fertilizer suppliers like Mosaic and CF Industries Holdings. The price of fertilizer has jumped in recent days, with the Middle East conflict choking off big chunks of the world's supply of ammonia, urea, sulfur and phosphates.

Meanwhile, financial and industrial shares tumbled. Energy-guzzling airlines, manufacturers and transportation firms in the industrial sector would be hard-hit by a prolonged oil shock. Shares of banks, brokerages and asset managers were weighed down further by a report that one Morgan Stanley private-credit fund is among the latest to say it won't give back all the money to investors wanting out.

Investors had a relatively muted reaction in the days immediately following the start of the U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran on Feb. 28. Investors were betting the conflict would be brief and contained — not unlike markets' reaction to the U.S. ousting of Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro earlier this year.

Now, worries are mounting that the war will last longer than expected, lifting energy costs and eating into corporate profits.

"We're at the stage now where it's not a certain disaster by any means," Grisanti said. "But all of the sudden, disaster becomes a part of the things we have to contemplate."

Also souring the mood are dwindling hopes for interest-rate cuts this year. Investors have drastically scaled back their bets: Interest-rate futures showed Thursday afternoon that traders see a 45% chance that the Federal Reserve doesn't cut rates at all in 2026, according to CME Group. That was up from 24% Wednesday and 4% before the Iran war started.

The shift in rate bets has led to a jump in short-term Treasury yields, which are particularly sensitive to the near-term outlook for monetary policy. The yield on the 2-year Treasury note settled Thursday at 3.759%, according to Tradeweb, its biggest one-day gain since May of last year.

"There is a growing sense of unease," said Tim Thomas, chief investment officer at Badgley Phelps. "The longer this goes on, the more likely we see a drag on global growth."

Write to Hannah Erin Lang at hannaherin.lang@wsj.com

data.title: Once Upon a Farm Guides for Slower Growth This Year
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Once Upon a Farm forecast slower growth this year after expanded distribution and higher prices helped results in its latest quarter.

The guidance and results come after the maker of organic children's food, co-founded by veteran food executive John Foraker and actress Jennifer Garner, made its stock market debut last month.

Once Upon a Farm on Thursday guided for sales to grow 25% to 29% to a range of $302 million to $310 million this year, compared with 54% growth in 2025. Analysts polled by FactSet expected sales of $303.6 million.

Chief Executive Foraker said the company is looking to accelerate its growth initiatives following its initial public offering.

"Parents today are more committed than ever to providing their children with the highest quality, organic nutrition, and Once Upon a Farm is uniquely positioned to capitalize on this demand to drive sustained growth in 2026 and beyond," Foraker said.

The forecast came as the company swung to a fourth-quarter profit as revenue climbed double-digits.

The company posted a fourth-quarter profit of $22.5 million, or 11 cents a share, compared with a loss of $12.3 million, or $1.88 a share, a year earlier. Analysts projected earnings of 10 cents a share.

Revenue climbed 30% to $64 million, compared with analyst estimates of $63 million.

The growth was driven by broadened distribution and higher average selling prices, the company said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Stereotaxis 10-K: $32.4M Revenue, $(0.25) EPS
data.description:
Stereotaxis reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $32.4 million, up 20% year-over-year, driven by higher system sales and a full-year contribution from the APT acquisition. The company posted a net loss of $21.6 million, or $(0.25) per share, an improvement from the prior year loss and $(0.30) per share. Gross margin was roughly stable at 53% as product mix shifted toward disposables.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $32.4 million, up 20% vs. $26.9 million in 2024.
  • Gross profit (Gross margin): $17.1 million, 53% of revenue (vs. $14.6 million, 54% in 2024).
  • Operating loss (Operating income): $(22.1) million, improved from $(24.7) million in 2024.
  • Net loss (Net income): $(21.6) million, improved from $(24.0) million in 2024.
  • Net loss per share (Basic/Diluted): $(0.25), improved from $(0.30) in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and recurring sales: Growth driven by higher system sales and expanded disposables/recurring revenue following the APT catheter acquisition; disposables increased ~21% to $22.2 million.
  • Product and regulatory progress: GenesisX achieved CE Mark in 2024 and FDA 510(k) clearance in 2025; MAGiC and MAGiC Sweep received FDA/CE clearances in 2025–2026, supporting broader market access.
  • Backlog and install cadence: System backlog stood at $9.1 million as of 12/31/2025, with approximately 78% expected to convert in 2026; a shorter GenesisX install cycle is improving accessibility.
  • Operational integration and supply risks: Management is integrating APT and scaling catheter manufacturing amid supply chain pressures, including prior uncertainty around J&J catheter supply.

Original SEC Filing: Stereotaxis, Inc. [ STXS ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Rapid Micro Biosystems 2025 10-K: Revenue $33.6M, Net loss $47.1M
data.description:
Rapid Micro Biosystems reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $33.6 million, up 19.7% year-over-year, while recording a net loss of $47.1 million, essentially flat with the prior year. Gross profit improved to $1.0 million with a 3.1% gross margin versus a negative margin in 2024, reflecting margin recovery despite continued net losses. The company is scaling operations and commercial initiatives to convert system placements into recurring consumables and service revenue.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $33.6 million, increase of $5.5 million (19.7%) vs 2024.
  • Gross profit: $1.0 million; gross margin 3.1% for 2025 (improved from (0.4)% in 2024).
  • Net loss (Net Income): $(47.1) million, essentially flat vs $(46.9) million in 2024.
  • Operating Income: Not provided in the supplied data.
  • Net loss per share (Net Income Per Share): Not provided in the supplied data.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Product and service revenue rose 19.7% to $33.6 million, driven by higher system placements and increased consumable volumes.
  • Channel and partnership expansion: Entered a global co-exclusive distribution agreement with MilliporeSigma in 2025 to expand industrial quality-control reach, including committed initial multi-system purchases.
  • Adoption and placements: Placed 190 systems (up 17.3% year-over-year) with 155 systems validated for customer use.
  • Customer footprint: Serving 75% of the top 20 pharmaceutical companies and major cell and gene therapy manufacturers.
  • Operational scaling: Investing in manufacturing, service, and R&D capabilities and launching a commercial rapid sterility application while scaling related production.
  • Recurring revenue mix: Recurring revenue increased to $17.8 million, representing 53.1% of total revenue, reflecting growth in consumables and service contracts from validated systems.

Original SEC Filing: RAPID MICRO BIOSYSTEMS, INC. [ RPID ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Acacia Research 2025 10-K: $285.2M Revenue, $21.7M Net Income
data.description:
Acacia Research reported total revenues of $285.2 million for the fiscal year, a 133% increase driven by IP license fees and contributions from recent acquisitions, and recorded net income attributable to Acacia Research of $21.7 million. Operating income improved to $6.4 million and income before taxes was $31.3 million, with income tax expense of $6.8 million impacting the effective tax rate. The results reflect a shift toward larger paid-up IP license deals and stronger performance from acquired manufacturing and energy businesses.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenues: $285.2 million, increase of $162.9 million (133%) versus 2024.
  • Operating income (loss): $6.4 million, an improvement of $39.3 million versus 2024 (prior year loss $32.9 million).
  • Income before income taxes: $31.3 million, versus a loss of $38.1 million in 2024.
  • Income tax (expense) benefit: $(6.8) million, a swing of $(10.3) million from 2024.
  • Net income (loss) attributable to Acacia Research: $21.7 million, improvement of $57.7 million versus 2024 (prior year loss $36.1 million).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and channel shift: Intellectual Property revenue moved toward larger paid-up license deals; Manufacturing shifted toward higher-margin consumables.
  • Acquisitions and integration: Completed acquisitions including Deflecto and Benchmark, scaling the manufacturing and energy platforms and integrating global manufacturing sites for full-year contributions.
  • IP portfolio activity: Executed intake of a Wi‑Fi 7 patent portfolio and expanded licensing activity, bringing the company to more than 1,600 license agreements to date.
  • Energy operations: Expanded oil & gas acreage to approximately 155,000 net acres, supporting the company’s energy platform growth.
  • Operational momentum: Continued scaling of manufacturing operations and consumables businesses, supporting higher-margin product mix and expected ongoing contributions from recent acquisitions.

Original SEC Filing: ACACIA RESEARCH CORP [ ACTG ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Hungary returns seized Ukrainian bank vehicles, withholds cash and gold
data.description:
Hungary returned two seized armoured bank vehicles to Ukraine on Thursday but withheld cash and gold worth approximately $82 million, citing an investigation into alleged money laundering, a move Kyiv has denounced as theft.

Relations between Hungary and Ukraine, already strained by Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban's close ties to Russia, reached a new low last week when Hungary detained seven Ukrainians transporting cash and gold.

Kyiv accused Budapest of taking the bank employees hostage in order to pressure Ukraine into restarting suspended oil shipments. It said the employees were engaged in a legitimate transfer.

Those detained were expelled by Hungary and crossed into Ukraine on Friday.

Ukraine's Oschadbank said the vehicles were turned over to diplomats and customs officers but that they had sustained some damage which would be formally assessed once they were back in Ukraine.

The bank said the cash and gold remained "illegally held in Hungary."

Ukraine has demanded the return of the seized assets, with Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha on Sunday describing Hungary's actions as an "unprecedented act of state banditism and racketeering."

The detainees' lawyer, Lorant Horvath, told reporters on Thursday that the Ukrainian bank believed the money transport was "entirely lawful."

"Oschadbank will use all legal means at its disposal to recover its assets," he said.

Hungary's parliament passed a law proposed by Orban's Fidesz Party on Tuesday, allowing the tax authority to retain the cash and gold for 60 days while it investigates the origin and destination of the assets and evaluates any implications for Hungary's national security.

The tensions come as Orban faces a serious electoral challenge to his 16-year rule on April 12. He has made the war in Ukraine a central plank of his campaign, saying the centre-right opposition would drag Hungary into the conflict, which the opposition has repeatedly denied.

Orban has also vetoed new EU sanctions on Moscow as well as a huge loan for Ukraine over the oil dispute.

data.title: GoPro 10-K: Revenue $651.5M, EPS (loss) $(0.59) — FY2025 results
data.description:
GoPro reported FY2025 revenue of $651.5 million and a basic and diluted net loss per share of $(0.59), reflecting softer consumer demand and fewer camera units shipped. The company narrowed its net loss to $(93.5) million from $(432.3) million in FY2024, while operating losses persisted amid restructuring and a goodwill impairment. Revenue declines and margin pressure prompted cost reduction and R&D prioritization ahead of a product rollout planned for 2Q26.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $651.5 million, down 19% year-over-year (FY2025 vs FY2024).
  • Revenue by category: Hardware $545.3 million; Subscription and services $106.3 million.
  • Gross profit: $219.2 million; gross margin 33.6% (FY2025), down 20 basis points versus FY2024.
  • Operating loss: $(83.3) million; operating expenses totaled $302.5 million (46% of revenue), including an $18.6 million goodwill impairment.
  • Net loss: $(93.5) million (improved versus $(432.3) million in FY2024, which included a $294.9 million deferred tax valuation allowance).
  • Basic and diluted net loss per share: $(0.59) in FY2025, versus $(2.82) in FY2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend and demand: FY2025 revenue declined 19% driven by approximately 25% fewer camera units shipped and softer consumer demand.
  • Sales channels: Retail remained the primary channel (74% of revenue) while sales through GoPro.com grew to 26% of revenue.
  • Subscription performance: Subscription attach rose to 43% in Q4 2025; annual retention remained around 68%. AI training opt-in for subscribers was launched in 2025.
  • Product launches: Introduced the MAX2 (8K 360) and LIT HERO in 2025; continued support of HERO13 Black variants and modular accessories to reinforce premium positioning.
  • Operational actions & outlook: Implemented cost reductions and prioritized R&D for a GP3 product rollout targeted for 2Q26, with tightened inventory and production discipline to help restore profitability.

Original SEC Filing: GoPro, Inc. [ GPRO ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Banks Prepare $7.15 Billion Debt Deal for Sealed Air Buyout
data.description:
Banks are preparing a sizeable financing package that could soon test investor appetite for leveraged buyout debt. Lenders including JPMorgan Chase & Co. are working on a roughly $7.15 billion debt offering to help fund Clayton Dubilier & Rice's planned acquisition of Sealed Air . According to people familiar with the matter, banks have begun gauging demand among institutional investors for a mix of leveraged loans and high-yield bonds that could potentially launch as soon as next week, though discussions are ongoing and the timing and structure could still change.

The proposed financing is expected to include several layers of debt tied to the transaction. A filing indicates the package could feature a $4.3 billion leveraged loan alongside a euro-denominated loan equivalent to about $600 million. The remainder of the structure is expected to include secured bonds divided between a $1.15 billion US-dollar tranche and a euro tranche equivalent to roughly $600 million, as well as about $500 million of unsecured notes, according to the filing.

The deal could become another important gauge of demand in the leveraged finance market after recent volatility made it more challenging for banks to distribute large amounts of buyout debt to investors. Market participants are also watching another potential test: a multibillion-dollar financing package tied to the record leveraged buyout of Electronic Arts , which could also come to market as soon as next week.

data.title: SentinelOne Posts Higher Rev, Wider Loss in 4Q
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

SentinelOne reported a 20% jump in revenue, but posted a steeper loss in the fourth quarter.

The cybersecurity company on Thursday reported a loss of $110.2 million, or 33 cents a share, compared with a loss of $70.8 million, or 22 cents a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, the company reported adjusted earnings of 7 cents a share. Analysts surveyed by FactSet were expecting 6 cents a share.

Revenue rose to $271.2 million, from $225.5 million a year earlier, and in line with the expectations of analysts polled by FactSet. Operating expenses rose to $276.7 million from $248.8 million.

For the current first quarter, the company is expecting revenue between $276 million and $278 million, with adjusted earnings between 1 cent and 2 cents a share.

Analysts expect revenue of $277.3 million and 5 cents a share in adjusted earnings for the first quarter.

The company is forecasting full-year revenue between $1.2 billion and $1.21 billion, with adjusted earnings between 32 cents and 38 cents a share.

Analysts expect $1.2 billion in revenue and adjusted earnings of 30 cents a share for the full year.

SentinelOne's stock fell 5% to $13.07 in after-hours trading.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Adobe Posts Higher Sales With CEO Set to Depart
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Adobe said Chief Executive Shantanu Narayen, credited with leading the software company's transformation over the past 18 years, will step down after it finds a new leader for the AI era.

Adobe announced Narayen's plans to depart as it posted higher sales in its first quarter. Shares fell 6% to $252.98 in after-hours trading.

Narayen, who joined Adobe in 1998, will stay on as chair of the board. His departure comes amid market jitters that artificial intelligence will upend most software providers.

Adobe also posted a profit of $1.89 billion, or $4.60 a share, compared with $1.81 billion, or $4.14 a share, a year earlier.

Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted per-share earnings were $6.06, ahead of the $5.87 anticipated by analysts, according to FactSet.

Revenue rose 12% to $6.40 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet forecast revenue of $6.28 billion.

Adobe's AI-first annualized recurring revenue more than tripled year over year, Narayen said. The company ended the quarter with $26.06 billion in ARR, in line with Wall Street's estimate.

Subscription revenue increased 13% year over year.

In the current second quarter, Adobe expects revenue will be between $6.43 billion and $6.48 billion, with adjusted earnings per share in the range of $5.80 to $5.85. Analysts are projecting $6.43 billion in sales and $5.68 a share in adjusted earnings.

Adobe reaffirmed its full-year guidance.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Diversified Energy Files 8K - Entry Into Definitive Agreement >DEC
data.description:
Diversified Energy Co. (DEC) filed a Form 8K - Entry Into a Definitive Agreement - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 12, 2026.

On March 9, 2026, Diversified Energy Company (the "Company") entered into an Underwriting Agreement (the "Underwriting Agreement") with certain affiliates of EIG Global Energy Partners (the "Selling Stockholders") and Citigroup Global Markets Inc. (the "Underwriter"), relating to the offer and sale (the "Offering") of 7,501,585 shares (the "Shares") of the Company's common stock, par value $0.01 per share (the "Common Stock"), by the Selling Stockholders.

On March 11, 2026, the Selling Stockholders completed the Offering of the 7,501,585 shares of Common Stock, which includes 3,750,000 shares of Common Stock that were repurchased by the Company at a price per share equal to the price at which the Underwriter purchased the shares from the Selling Stockholders in the Offering. The closing of the share repurchase occurred substantially concurrently with closing of the Offering. The Company did not sell any shares of Common Stock in the Offering and did not receive any proceeds from the sale of the Shares offered by the Selling Stockholders.

The Underwriting Agreement contains customary representations and warranties, agreements and obligations, closing conditions and termination provisions. The Company and the Selling Stockholders have agreed to indemnify the Underwriter against certain liabilities, including liabilities under the Securities Act of 1933, as amended, and to contribute to payments the Underwriter may be required to make in the event of any such liabilities.

The foregoing description of the Underwriting Agreement is not complete and is qualified in its entirety by reference to the copy of the Underwriting Agreement, which is filed herewith as Exhibit 1.1 and incorporated into this Item 1.01 by reference.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1922446/000192244626000031/dec-20260309.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1922446/000192244626000031/0001922446-26-000031-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: BMNM: Q4 net income was $3.4M, book value per share rose to $1.26, and a $2.5M buyback was announced
data.description:
Net income reached $3.4 million for Q4 and $5.8 million for 2025, with book value per share rising to $1.26. A $2.5 million share repurchase plan and the acquisition of 80% of TJIM were announced, while market outlook is clouded by geopolitical risks.

Original document: Bimini Capital Management, Inc. [BMNM] SEC 8-K Current Report — Mar. 13 2026

data.title: Pop culture collectibles maker Funko tops estimates for Q4 net sales
data.description:
Overview

  • Funko's Q4 net sales fell 7% but beat analyst expectations

  • Company cited strong sales of entertainment properties and Bitty Pop! for Q4 performance

  • Company reduced inventory and paid down $16 mln of debt in Q4

Outlook

  • Funko sees 2026 net sales flat to up 3% vs 2025

  • Company expects 2026 gross margin of 41% to 43%

  • Funko forecasts 2026 adjusted EBITDA of $70 mln to $80 mln

Result Drivers

  • ENTERTAINMENT PROPERTIES - Co said strong sales of KPop Demon Hunters, Stranger Things, Bitty Pop! and Pop! Yourself in Europe drove Q4 performance

  • CATEGORY MIX - Loungefly sales rose 1.8% while core collectibles fell 5% and 'Other' dropped 51.7% in Q4

data.title: National Beverage Q3: $264.6M Revenue, $0.44 EPS
data.description:
National Beverage reported third-quarter net sales of $264.6 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.44, with net income of $41.2 million for the period. Revenue declined modestly year-over-year as case volumes fell while average selling prices rose, and profitability held steady with gross margin improving slightly. The company cited higher packaging costs offset by pricing actions and maintained operating expense stability.

Financial Highlights

  • Net sales: $264.6 million (Q3 FY2026), decreased 0.9% year-over-year.
  • Gross profit: $99.6 million (Q3 FY2026); gross margin 37.6% versus 37.1% year-over-year.
  • Operating income: $51.1 million (Q3 FY2026), largely unchanged from $50.6 million prior year.
  • Net income: $41.2 million (Q3 FY2026), up from $39.6 million year-over-year; effective tax rate ~23.6%.
  • Net income per share: Diluted EPS $0.44 (Q3 FY2026); basic also $0.44. Nine months diluted EPS $1.53 with nine months net income $143.3 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Net sales decline was driven by approximately 4.8%–4.9% lower case volumes, partially offset by roughly 4.4%–4.7% higher average selling prices.
  • Distribution and seasonality: Continued U.S.-focused hybrid distribution model combining warehouse shipments and direct-store-delivery; summer seasonality influenced volumes.
  • Brand performance: Power+ portfolio (LaCroix, Clear Fruit, Rip It, Everfresh) and legacy sodas (Shasta, Faygo) experienced volume declines, though pricing improvements supported margin resilience.
  • Costs and productivity: Packaging and ingredient costs increased, raising average cost per case by about 3.4%–3.7%; capital projects are underway aimed at expanding capacity and improving efficiency.
  • Liquidity and operations: Cash build and stronger working capital enhanced operational flexibility, supporting ongoing production and marketing programs.

Original SEC Filing: NATIONAL BEVERAGE CORP [ FIZZ ] - 10-Q - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.
